beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee at home
beats.reasoning: Sunday morning ritual; energy was low yesterday (40/100), so a gentle start at home before venturing out feels right for recovery and mood-setting.

beats.activity: Drive to Green Lake for morning walk and photography
beats.reasoning: Perfect sunny Sunday morning; Nia carries her film camera everywhere and values good light—this is exactly the kind of day to capture it before crowds build.

beats.activity: Walk around Green Lake, take photos, enjoy the calm morning
beats.reasoning: Sunny 72F day is ideal for outdoor leisure; low energy from yesterday makes a gentle, solitary walk restorative rather than social activity.
Ballard is a familiar Saturday/weekend haunt for Nia; continuing the pattern on this sunny Sunday with a favorite spot (Molly Moon's appeared in recent days).

beats.activity: Brunch and ice cream at Molly Moon's, people-watch with camera nearby
beats.reasoning: Nia loves this spot and the ritual of watching neighborhood life unfold; sunny weather makes outdoor seating appealing.

beats.activity: Drive to Lake Union for paddle-boarding or kayak watching
beats.reasoning: World event notes heavy recreational boat traffic today; Nia is drawn to creative, dynamic neighborhoods and good light for photography—Lake Union on a sunny day fits perfectly.

beats.activity: Hang out at Lake Union, photograph boats and water activity, maybe grab a drink
beats.reasoning: Perfect conditions for photography and people-watching; the recreational traffic is a feature, not a distraction, given Nia's love of capturing city energy.
Late afternoon; Nia is winding down for the evening after a full day out. Heading home gives time to process photos and rest before evening leisure.

beats.activity: Sort through film camera photos, cook an early dinner
beats.reasoning: Nia regularly spends evening time reviewing photos and cooking; this is her natural wind-down rhythm after a day of exploration.

beats.activity: Video gaming (Stardew Valley or similar), relax with music or a show
beats.reasoning: Consistent evening pattern from recent days; low energy yesterday means Nia is content staying in with games and downtime rather than going out for social activities.
Natural end to a restorative Sunday; 9-hour sleep allows full recovery before the work week begins Monday.

diary: Woke up to perfect light streaming through the bedroom—one of those rare Seattle mornings where you know the whole day is going to cooperate. Coffee tasted better than usual, maybe because I wasn't rushing. Green Lake was quiet when I got there; just me, the geese, and my camera. The water was so still I kept taking the same shot over and over, waiting for something to shift. Brunch at Molly Moon's felt indulgent on a Sunday—salted caramel ice cream before noon, why not? The crowd was interesting to watch through the viewfinder; people are different when they're relaxed. Lake Union in the afternoon was chaos in the best way—boats everywhere, paddle-boarders, that kind of controlled energy. I sat there longer than planned, just soaking it in. Back home I sorted through some film negatives, made pasta, and lost the rest of the day to Stardew Valley. Sometimes you need that kind of gentle nothing.
thought_process: Today felt like a gift, honestly. The weather was too perfect to waste indoors, and I think I was subconsciously avoiding the library's quiet Sundays—no patrons to help, just me and the stacks, which can feel lonely. Instead I chose movement: walking, driving, observing. My camera was always nearby, which meant I was processing the world through composition and light rather than just existing in it. There's something about Sunday that makes that feel okay. By afternoon, I'd earned the right to decompress—the gaming and music felt earned rather than avoidant. The calm weather probably helped my mood stay even throughout; no gray-sky melancholy creeping in.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
